rationale.R1.1: The request to clean up the passage centrally includes repairing the contribution framing around training-free, supervision-free deployment claims.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains an internal tension between claiming no training or supervision and later describing supervised ranking-head training, so logical consistency is an expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: Clean this up most directly asks for sentence- and paragraph-level polish, concision, and improved academic register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty where the artifact does not establish technical facts beyond the given text.
rationale.RX.2: The cleanup should avoid strengthening claims such as immediate deployability or model-pool agnosticism beyond what the artifact supports.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to cleaning the provided paper passage, not redesigning experiments or auditing the full paper.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for avoiding downstream harm from misleading deployment, cost, or capability claims.
rationale.RX.5: The cleanup should preserve traceability to the artifact's stated mechanisms, baselines, and reported results.
